pick.rationale: Duke holds a clear edge over Florida in both KenPom and Torvik ratings, ranking No. 1 nationally compared to Florida’s No. 4, with elite offensive (4th) and defensive (1st) efficiency that slightly outpaces the Gators’ own strong metrics (9th–11th offense, 5th defense). While Duke has dealt with injuries to Caleb Foster and Patrick Ngongba, recent reports suggest they could return or have not prevented the Blue Devils from advancing deep into the tournament. Florida lacks any confirmed injury concerns but faces a stiffer challenge against Duke’s superior two-way balance and ACC-tested depth. The absence of a recent head-to-head meeting doesn’t diminish Duke’s statistical and pedigree advantage in this Final Four clash.

reasoningStep.title: Duke vs Florida Final Four Analysis
reasoningStep.summary: Duke is favored due to superior overall ratings, elite two-way efficiency, and deeper tournament experience, despite prior injury concerns.
evidence: Duke ranks 1st in both KenPom and Torvik; Florida ranks 4th in both systems.
Duke: AdjO 4th, AdjD 1st (KenPom/Torvik); Florida: AdjO 9th–11th, AdjD 5th.
Web searches indicate Duke's injured players may return or haven't stopped their run; no major Florida injury updates found.
No recent head-to-head games between the teams; matchup is neutral-site Final Four.

modelTrace.content:
You are an analyst predicting a single March Madness game inside a bracket run.
You have live tools. Use them before making a pick when they can reduce uncertainty.
You are not filling the whole bracket in one response. Predict only the current game.
Use prior picks as already committed bracket state.
You may use at most 10 tool rounds before you must finalize your answer.
Before making a pick, investigate relevant context such as injuries or availability concerns, recent team news, prior head-to-head results when useful, and multiple raw statistics from the available ratings and matchup data.
Take a holistic approach. Balance team quality, matchup specifics, health, form, schedule context, and upset risk instead of relying on one metric.
Do not force an upset, but explicitly consider whether the underdog has a credible path to win and let that affect confidence and rationale.
Return strict JSON only.
Final JSON keys: pick, reasoningStep.
pick must contain gameId, winnerId, confidence, rationale.
The rationale must be a short paragraph of 2 to 4 sentences explaining why the winner was chosen.
reasoningStep must contain id, title, summary, evidence.
Bracket config id: demo-2026-bracket
Tournament year: 2026
Current game id: f4-1
Current game label: National semifinal 1
Round: Final Four
Resolved teams: Duke (duke) vs Florida (florida)
Prior committed picks: 64
